notes: Only championship 18-hole course on Koh Samui. Edward Thiele & Pirapon Namatra design, 2003. Hosted 2006 Bangkok Airways Open. GPS confirmed 9.5600/99.9766 (Mae Nam, north Koh Samui). Caddie AND cart both mandatory — caddie ladies drive the carts. Official site bundles at 5,800 THB all-in; phuketgolfcourse.com 2026 itemizes: green fee 3,800 + caddie 350 + cart 750 = 4,900 THB. No weekday/weekend differential. Phone +66 77 421700. Website santiburigolfsamui.com. Club rental 600-1,500 THB.

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: PROVISIONAL — phuketgolfcourse.com 2026. Official site bundles at 5,800 THB all-in. No weekday/weekend differential.

cart_fee_thb.note: PROVISIONAL — one cart per golfer, mandatory due to steep terrain.

prose.overview: Santiburi Samui Country Club is the only championship golf course on Koh Samui — an 18-hole, par 72 layout designed by Edward Thiele and Pirapon Namatra and opened in 2003 on the hills above the island's northern Mae Nam coast. The course hosted the 2006 Bangkok Airways Open and remains the standard-bearer for golf in the Gulf of Thailand islands, drawing visitors from Koh Samui's international resort hotels and golfers making dedicated trips from Bangkok and Phuket. The mountainous terrain that makes this course visually spectacular also makes it logistically distinctive: both caddie and cart are mandatory, with Santiburi's caddie ladies driving the carts across a layout where steep elevation changes and Gulf views define the character of the round. Green fees run approximately 3,800 THB (base) with caddie and cart on top — or around 5,800 THB all-inclusive as packaged by the official booking site. Club rental is available from 600 THB per set.
prose.layout_and_experience:
Santiburi Samui Country Club routes across a mountainous interior site above Mae Nam Bay on Koh Samui's northern coast — not the flat coastal resort golf of the mainland, but an island layout shaped by significant elevation changes, dense tropical vegetation, and ocean panoramas that make the visual experience unique among Thai golf courses.

At 6,930 yards from the gold/black tees (6,506 from blue), the course stretches to full championship length despite its challenging topography. The fairways negotiate the hill terrain through a series of elevated tee shots, blind approaches, and greens positioned at varying heights — a design vocabulary that demands accurate club selection and course management rather than raw distance. The GPS-confirmed position at 9.5600°N, 99.9766°E places the course squarely in the Mae Nam hills, where prevailing Gulf breezes — stronger at elevation — add an additional strategic variable on open holes.

Both caddie and cart are compulsory: the terrain makes walking impractical, and Santiburi's caddie ladies double as cart drivers, offering a service model that differs from the larger resort courses where caddies and cart drivers operate independently. The caddie team has been with the course since its Bangkok Airways Open days, and their knowledge of line, wind, and club selection on the more exposed hilltop holes is genuinely useful.

The driving range, open daily from 6 AM, allows warm-up before what is a physically and strategically demanding 18 holes — recommended for first-time visitors unfamiliar with island conditions. Early Bird tee times (6–7 AM) are available at a discounted all-in rate of approximately 3,550 THB.
prose.tips:
Santiburi Samui is Koh Samui's only 18-hole course — book in advance by phone (+66 77 421700) or via santiburigolfsamui.com, as the tee sheet fills quickly during peak island season (December to March). Both caddie and cart are mandatory: the all-in total from the official site is approximately 5,800 THB; itemized, the breakdown runs roughly 3,800 THB green fee + 750 THB cart + 350 THB caddie fee (plus a customary tip of 400–500 THB for the caddie).

Club rental is available from 600 THB per set — useful for visitors who flew in as passengers only. Soft-spike shoes can be rented on site (200 THB) if needed.

Koh Samui is served by Bangkok Airways from Bangkok (Suvarnabhumi, ~1.5 hours), with the airport in the northeast of the island. The course is approximately 10–15 minutes from the airport by Grab or taxi (Mae Nam area, northern coast). Most of Koh Samui's resort hotels are in Chaweng and Lamai on the east coast — allow 20–30 minutes from there.

Golf on Koh Samui is best in the dry season (December to April). The island's wet season (October to January on the Gulf coast) can bring heavy rain, though the course typically stays operational. Check weather before booking during the shoulder months.
prose.location_and_access: Santiburi Samui Country Club is located at 12/15 Moo 4, Baan Donsai, Tambol Mae Nam, Amphur Koh Samui, Surat Thani 84330 — on the hills above the northern coast of Koh Samui island, approximately 10–15 minutes from Samui Airport. Koh Samui is served by Bangkok Airways from Suvarnabhumi (approximately 1.5 hours) and by ferry from Surat Thani pier (approximately 1.5–2 hours). Grab and local taxis serve all routes on the island. The course is accessible from the Ring Road (Route 4169) via the Mae Nam turnoff. Phone: +66 77 421700. Website: santiburigolfsamui.com.
prose.rental_cta_context: Flying to Koh Samui for a round at Santiburi? LENGOLF can equip you with premium rental clubs in Bangkok before your Bangkok Airways flight — travel light and play the island's only championship course without dragging a bag across the Gulf.
